name: getWellKnownAppAuthenticatorConfiguration
description: Retrieve the well-known app authenticator configuration. Retrieves the well-known app authenticator configuration. Includes an app authenticator's settings, supported methods, and other details.

name: listAuthenticators
description: List all authenticators. Lists all authenticators

name: createAuthenticator
description: Create an authenticator. Creates an authenticator

activate.description: Whether to execute the activation lifecycle operation when Okta creates the authenticator

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: getAuthenticator
description: Retrieve an authenticator. Retrieves an authenticator from your Okta organization by `authenticatorId`

name: replaceAuthenticator
description: Replace an authenticator. Replaces the properties for an authenticator identified by `authenticatorId`

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: listAllCustomAAGUIDs
description: List all custom AAGUIDs. Lists all custom Authenticator Attestation Global Unique Identifiers (AAGUIDs) in the org Only custom AAGUIDs that an admin has created are returned.

name: createCustomAAGUID
description: Create a custom AAGUID. Creates a custom AAGUID for the WebAuthn authenticator

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: getCustomAAGUID
description: Retrieve a custom AAGUID. Retrieves a custom AAGUID

aaguid.description: Unique ID of a custom AAGUID

name: replaceCustomAAGUID
description: Replace a custom AAGUID. Replaces a custom AAGUID for the specified WebAuthn authenticator

aaguid.description: Unique ID of a custom AAGUID

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: updateCustomAAGUID
description: Update a custom AAGUID. Updates the properties of a custom AAGUID by the `authenticatorId` and `aaguid` ID

aaguid.description: Unique ID of a custom AAGUID

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: deleteCustomAAGUID
description: Delete a custom AAGUID. Deletes a custom AAGUID You can only delete custom AAGUIDs that an admin has created.

aaguid.description: Unique ID of a custom AAGUID

name: activateAuthenticator
description: Activate an authenticator. Activates an authenticator by `authenticatorId`

name: deactivateAuthenticator
description: Deactivate an authenticator. Deactivates an authenticator by `authenticatorId`

name: listAuthenticatorMethods
description: List all methods of an authenticator. Lists all methods of an authenticator identified by `authenticatorId`

name: getAuthenticatorMethod
description: Retrieve an authenticator method. Retrieves a method identified by `methodType` of an authenticator identified by `authenticatorId`

name: replaceAuthenticatorMethod
description: Replace an authenticator method. Replaces a method of `methodType` for an authenticator identified by `authenticatorId`

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: activateAuthenticatorMethod
description: Activate an authenticator method. Activates a method for an authenticator identified by `authenticatorId` and `methodType`

name: deactivateAuthenticatorMethod
description: Deactivate an authenticator method. Deactivates a method for an authenticator identified by `authenticatorId` and `methodType`

name: verifyRpIdDomain
description: Verify a Relying Party ID domain. Verifies the [Relying Party identifier (RP ID)](https://www.w3.org/TR/webauthn/#relying-party-identifier) domain for the specified WebAuthn authenticator and the specific `webauthn` authenticator method

name: listBehaviorDetectionRules
description: List all behavior detection rules. Lists all behavior detection rules with pagination support

name: createBehaviorDetectionRule
description: Create a behavior detection rule. Creates a new behavior detection rule

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: getBehaviorDetectionRule
description: Retrieve a behavior detection rule. Retrieves a Behavior Detection Rule by `behaviorId`

behaviorId.description: ID of the Behavior Detection Rule

name: replaceBehaviorDetectionRule
description: Replace a behavior detection rule. Replaces a Behavior Detection Rule by `behaviorId`

behaviorId.description: ID of the Behavior Detection Rule

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: deleteBehaviorDetectionRule
description: Delete a behavior detection rule. Deletes a Behavior Detection Rule by `behaviorId`

behaviorId.description: ID of the Behavior Detection Rule

name: activateBehaviorDetectionRule
description: Activate a behavior detection rule. Activates a behavior detection rule

behaviorId.description: ID of the Behavior Detection Rule

name: deactivateBehaviorDetectionRule
description: Deactivate a behavior detection rule. Deactivates a behavior detection rule

behaviorId.description: ID of the Behavior Detection Rule

name: listCaptchaInstances
description: List all CAPTCHA instances. Lists all CAPTCHA instances with pagination support. A subset of CAPTCHA instances can be returned that match a supported filter expression or query.

name: createCaptchaInstance
description: Create a CAPTCHA instance. Creates a new CAPTCHA instance. Currently, an org can only configure a single CAPTCHA instance.

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: getCaptchaInstance
description: Retrieve a CAPTCHA instance. Retrieves the properties of a specified CAPTCHA instance

captchaId.description: The unique key used to identify your CAPTCHA instance

name: updateCaptchaInstance
description: Update a CAPTCHA instance. Partially updates the properties of a specified CAPTCHA instance

captchaId.description: The unique key used to identify your CAPTCHA instance

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: replaceCaptchaInstance
description: Replace a CAPTCHA instance. Replaces the properties for a specified CAPTCHA instance

captchaId.description: The unique key used to identify your CAPTCHA instance

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: deleteCaptchaInstance
description: Delete a CAPTCHA instance. Deletes a specified CAPTCHA instance > **Note:** If your CAPTCHA instance is still associated with your org, the request fails. You must first update your Org-wide CAPTCHA settings to remove the CAPTCHA instance.

captchaId.description: The unique key used to identify your CAPTCHA instance

name: listDeviceAssurancePolicies
description: List all device assurance policies. Lists all device assurance policies

name: createDeviceAssurancePolicy
description: Create a device assurance policy. Creates a new device assurance policy

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: getDeviceAssurancePolicy
description: Retrieve a device assurance policy. Retrieves a device assurance policy by `deviceAssuranceId`

deviceAssuranceId.description: Id of the device assurance policy

name: replaceDeviceAssurancePolicy
description: Replace a device assurance policy. Replaces a device assurance policy by `deviceAssuranceId`

deviceAssuranceId.description: Id of the device assurance policy

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: deleteDeviceAssurancePolicy
description: Delete a device assurance policy. Deletes a device assurance policy by `deviceAssuranceId`. If the device assurance policy is currently being used in the org Authentication Policies, the delete will not be allowed.

deviceAssuranceId.description: Id of the device assurance policy

name: listDevicePostureChecks
description: List all device posture checks. Lists all device posture checks

name: createDevicePostureCheck
description: Create a device posture check. Creates a device posture check

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: listDefaultDevicePostureChecks
description: List all default device posture checks. Lists all default device posture checks. Default device posture checks are defined by Okta. Their type will always be `BUILTIN`.

name: getDevicePostureCheck
description: Retrieve a device posture check. Retrieves a device posture check by `postureCheckId`

postureCheckId.description: ID of the device posture check

name: replaceDevicePostureCheck
description: Replace a device posture check. Replaces a device posture check by `postureCheckId`

postureCheckId.description: ID of the device posture check

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: deleteDevicePostureCheck
description: Delete a device posture check. Deletes a device posture check by `postureCheckId`. You can't delete the device posture check if it's used in a device assurance policy.

postureCheckId.description: ID of the device posture check

name: getOrgCaptchaSettings
description: Retrieve the org-wide CAPTCHA settings. Retrieves the CAPTCHA settings object for your organization > **Note**: If the current organization hasn't configured CAPTCHA Settings, the request returns an empty object.

name: replacesOrgCaptchaSettings
description: Replace the org-wide CAPTCHA settings. Replaces the CAPTCHA settings object for your organization > **Note**: You can disable CAPTCHA for your organization by setting `captchaId` and `enabledPages` to `null`.

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: deleteOrgCaptchaSettings
description: Delete the org-wide CAPTCHA settings. Deletes the CAPTCHA settings object for your organization

name: listPolicies
description: List all policies. Lists all policies with the specified type

type.description: Specifies the type of policy to return. The following policy types are available only with the Okta Identity Engine - `ACCESS_POLICY`, <x-lifecycle class="ea"></x-lifecycle> `DEVICE_SIGNAL_COLLECTION`, `PROFILE_ENROLLMENT`, `POST_AUTH_SESSION` and `ENTITY_RISK`.

status.description: Refines the query by the `status` of the policy - `ACTIVE` or `INACTIVE`

q.description: Refines the query by policy name prefix (startWith method) passed in as `q=string`

sortBy.description: Refines the query by sorting on the policy `name` in ascending order

limit.description: Defines the number of policies returned, see [Pagination](https://developer.okta.com/docs/api/#pagination)

resourceId.description: Reference to the associated authorization server

after.description: End page cursor for pagination, see [Pagination](https://developer.okta.com/docs/api/#pagination)

name: createPolicy
description: Create a policy. Creates a policy. There are many types of policies that you can create. See [Policies](https://developer.okta.com/docs/concepts/policies/) for an overview of the types of policies available and links to more indepth information.

activate.description: This query parameter is only valid for Classic Engine orgs.

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: createPolicySimulation
description: Create a policy simulation. Creates a policy or policy rule simulation. The access simulation evaluates policy and policy rules based on the existing policy rule configuration. The evaluation result simulates what the real-world authentication flow is and what policy rules have been applied or matched to the authentication flow.

expand.description: Use `expand=EVALUATED` to include a list of evaluated but not matched policies and policy rules. Use `expand=RULE` to include details about why a rule condition wasn't matched.

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: getPolicy
description: Retrieve a policy. Retrieves a policy

name: replacePolicy
description: Replace a policy. Replaces the properties of a policy identified by `policyId`

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: deletePolicy
description: Delete a policy. Deletes a policy

name: listPolicyApps
description: List all apps mapped to a policy. Lists all applications mapped to a policy identified by `policyId` > **Note:** Use [List all resources mapped to a Policy](https://developer.okta.com/docs/api/openapi/okta-management/management/tag/Policy/#tag/Policy/operation/listPolicyMappings) to list all applications mapped to a policy.

name: clonePolicy
description: Clone an existing policy. Clones an existing policy

name: activatePolicy
description: Activate a policy. Activates a policy

name: deactivatePolicy
description: Deactivate a policy. Deactivates a policy

name: listPolicyMappings
description: List all resources mapped to a policy. Lists all resources mapped to a policy identified by `policyId`

name: mapResourceToPolicy
description: Map a resource to a policy. Maps a resource to a policy identified by `policyId` > **Note:** Use the [Assign an app sign-in policy](https://developer.okta.com/docs/api/openapi/okta-management/management/tag/ApplicationPolicies/#tag/ApplicationPolicies/operation/assignApplicationPolicy) endpoint to assign an app sign-in policy to an app.

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: getPolicyMapping
description: Retrieve a policy resource mapping. Retrieves a resource mapping for a policy identified by `policyId` and `mappingId`

name: deletePolicyResourceMapping
description: Delete a policy resource mapping. Deletes the resource mapping for a policy identified by `policyId` and `mappingId`

name: listPolicyRules
description: List all policy rules. Lists all policy rules

limit.description: Defines the number of policy rules returned. See [Pagination](https://developer.okta.com/docs/api/#pagination).

name: createPolicyRule
description: Create a policy rule. Creates a policy rule > **Note:** You can't create additional rules for the `PROFILE_ENROLLMENT` or `POST_AUTH_SESSION` policies.

limit.description: Defines the number of policy rules returned. See [Pagination](https://developer.okta.com/docs/api/#pagination).

activate.description: Set this parameter to `false` to create an `INACTIVE` rule.

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: getPolicyRule
description: Retrieve a policy rule. Retrieves a policy rule

name: replacePolicyRule
description: Replace a policy rule. Replaces the properties for a policy rule identified by `policyId` and `ruleId`

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: deletePolicyRule
description: Delete a policy rule. Deletes a policy rule identified by `policyId` and `ruleId`

name: activatePolicyRule
description: Activate a policy rule. Activates a policy rule identified by `policyId` and `ruleId`

name: deactivatePolicyRule
description: Deactivate a policy rule. Deactivates a policy rule identified by `policyId` and `ruleId`

name: getCurrentConfiguration
description: Retrieve the ThreatInsight configuration. Retrieves the ThreatInsight configuration for the org

name: updateConfiguration
description: Update the ThreatInsight configuration. Updates the ThreatInsight configuration for the org

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: listNetworkZones
description: List all network zones. Lists all Network Zones with pagination. A subset of zones can be returned that match a supported filter expression or query. This operation requires URL encoding. For example, `filter=(id eq "nzoul0wf9jyb8xwZm0g3" or id eq "nzoul1MxmGN18NDQT0g3")` is encoded as `filter=%28id+eq+%22nzoul0wf9jyb8xwZm0g3%22+or+id+eq+%22nzoul1MxmGN18NDQT0g3%22%29`. Okta supports filtering on the `id`, `usage`, and `system` properties. See [Filter](https://developer.okta.com/docs/api/#filter…

name: createNetworkZone
description: Create a network zone. Creates a Network Zone * For an IP Network Zone, you must define either `gateways` or `proxies`. * For a Dynamic Network Zone, you must define at least one of the following: `asns`, `locations`, or `proxyType`. * For an Enhanced Dynamic Network Zone, you must define at least one of the following: `asns`, `locations`, or `ipServiceCategories`. > **Note:** To view all properties for an Enhanced Dynamic Network Zone, select `DYNAMIC_V2` from the `type` dropdown list.

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: getNetworkZone
description: Retrieve a network zone. Retrieves a Network Zone by `zoneId`

name: replaceNetworkZone
description:
Replace a network zone. Replaces a Network Zone by `zoneId`. The replaced Network Zone type must be the same as the existing type. You can replace the usage (`POLICY`, `BLOCKLIST`) of a Network Zone by updating the `usage` attribute. **IP exempt zone**
If you have the IP exempt zone feature enabled, you can allow traffic from specific gateway IPs irrespective of Okta ThreatInsight configurations, blocked network zones, or IP change events within Identity Threat Protection with Okta AI.
…

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: deleteNetworkZone
description: Delete a network zone. Deletes a Network Zone by `zoneId` > **Notes:** > * You can't delete a Network Zone that's used by a [Policy](https://developer.okta.com/docs/api/openapi/okta-management/management/tag/Policy/) or [Rule](https://developer.okta.com/docs/api/openapi/okta-management/management/tag/Policy/#tag/Policy/operation/listPolicyRules). > * For Okta Identity Engine orgs, you can't delete a Network Zone with an ACTIVE `status`. <x-lifecycle class="oie"></x-lifecycle>

name: activateNetworkZone
description: Activate a network zone. Activates a Network Zone by `zoneId`

name: deactivateNetworkZone
description: Deactivate a network zone. Deactivates a Network Zone by `zoneId`

name: getAuthenticatorSettings
description: Retrieve the authenticator settings. Retrieves the Authenticator Settings for an org

name: replaceAuthenticatorSettings
description: Replace the authenticator settings. Replaces the Authenticator Settings for an org

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: getUserLockoutSettings
description: Retrieve the user lockout settings. Retrieves the User Lockout Settings for an org

name: replaceUserLockoutSettings
description: Replace the user lockout settings. Replaces the User Lockout Settings for an org

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: activatePreregistrationEnrollment
description: Activate a preregistered WebAuthn factor. Activates a preregistered WebAuthn factor. As part of this operation, Okta first decrypts and verifies the factor PIN and enrollment data sent by the fulfillment provider.

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: enrollPreregistrationEnrollment
description: Enroll a preregistered WebAuthn factor. Enrolls a preregistered WebAuthn factor. This WebAuthn factor has a longer challenge timeout period to accommodate the fulfillment request process. As part of this operation, Okta generates elliptic curve (EC) key-pairs used to encrypt the factor PIN and enrollment data sent by the fulfillment provider.

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: generateFulfillmentRequest
description: Generate a fulfillment request. Generates a fulfillment request by sending a WebAuthn preregistration event to start the flow. The WebAuthn preregistration integration for Okta Workflows uses a preregistration event to populate the fulfillment request.

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: sendPin
description: Send a PIN to user. Sends the decoded PIN for the specified WebAuthn preregistration enrollment. PINs are sent to the user's email. To resend the PIN, call this operation again.

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: listWebAuthnPreregistrationFactors
description: List all WebAuthn preregistration factors. Lists all WebAuthn preregistration factors for the specified user

userId.description: ID of an existing Okta user

name: deleteWebAuthnPreregistrationFactor
description: Delete a WebAuthn preregistration factor. Deletes a specific WebAuthn preregistration factor for a user

userId.description: ID of an existing Okta user

authenticatorEnrollmentId.description: ID for a WebAuthn preregistration factor in Okta

name: assignFulfillmentErrorWebAuthnPreregistrationFactor
description: Assign the fulfillment error status to a WebAuthn preregistration factor. Assigns the fulfillment error status to a WebAuthn preregistration factor for a user. The `/mark-error` path indicates that the specific `FULFILLMENT_ERRORED` AuthFactor status is set on the enrollment.

userId.description: ID of an existing Okta user

authenticatorEnrollmentId.description: ID for a WebAuthn preregistration factor in Okta
